---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size75[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size45[

Work on Paper 1

+ Choose Cases, 

+ Clarify key components, and

+ Plan next steps

]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Paper 1 description on Moodle Assignment



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

## This Week: Paper 1
.size40[
+ Write a report introducing us to the specific, local environmental problem you intend to target this semester
]

## For Today
.size40[
+ Bring to class a news story or piece of evidence describing a local environmental problem

    + Describe the problem and identify the key stakeholders
]

???

EVERYBODY READY TO GO WITH THEIR CASES?

<br>

Ok, here's what we need to keep in mind in order to give useful feedback on the cases today.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.center[.size55[**Solving a Local Environmental Problem**]]

<br>

.size40[
Paper 1: Present the basics of your problem

Paper 2: Analyze the complicating factors

Paper 3: Propose a policy to address the problem
]

???

Your projects this term are represented by three argument papers.

This week we start building and writing the first one.

SLIDE: Details on paper 1


---

class: middle, slideblue

### Paper 1: Write a report introducing us to the specific, local environmental problem you intend to target this semester

.size30[
Be sure to clarify the following elements and support your claims with evidence:

- Why is this an "environmental" issue?

- Why is this a "problem"? Be specific about who it impacts and in what ways.

- What are the causes of the problem you want to focus on?

- What data is available to allow us to measure the problem?
]

???

I'll post this description on the Moodle assignment.

ANY QUESTIONS ON THE ASSIGNMENT?

<br>

Ok, let's hear and discuss the cases you brought today.

As we listen let's try to help each other think about if this case lends itself well to our work this semester.

Given the 3 papers you have to write is this a good choice? Why or why not?

- Is it specific enough?

*Present and discuss each*



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size60[**For Thursday**]

.size40[
1. Choose your environmental problem,

2. Start outlining your paper, and

3. Find us data to measure the problem!
]

???

What kind of data do you need to measure this? How many different ways could we measure this?

Assignment for Tuesday: Go find us that data

QUESTIONS?
